mnemonics: CIA (Consistency, Integrity, Availability) - The core goals of a distributed system, just like the secret agency's core mission is security.
keyPoints: Continuous Internal Evaluation (CIE) is an ongoing assessment method used to monitor and ensure the health and performance of a distributed system.
It involves automated checks, metrics collection (e.g., latency, throughput), and logging to detect faults, performance degradation, or security breaches in real-time.
The goal of CIE is to maintain system reliability, availability, and consistency by enabling proactive responses to issues before they cause major failures.
commonMistakes: Confusing CIE with a one-time deployment test; it is a continuous process that runs for the entire system lifecycle.
Focusing only on high-level application metrics and ignoring lower-level system metrics like network latency or node resource utilization, which are often the root cause of problems.
Assuming a 'set and forget' configuration for monitoring thresholds instead of continuously adapting them as system workload and architecture evolve.
